commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a focused Telegram dispatch regression test for streamed reasoning plus final-answer delivery in a forum topic.

PR surface: Tests +52. Total +52 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. The source path is clear and the PR adds a focused synthetic regression, but the proof does not exercise a real Telegram forum topic.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof for a Telegram-visible behavior, not a safe autonomous code repair.

target.title: test(telegram): add regression test for forum topic message_thread_id with streamed reasoning
target.body:
## Root Cause

After thorough code tracing of all delivery paths in the current main branch (draft streams, `deliverInboundReplyWithMessageSendContext`, `deliverReplies`, `emitPreviewFinalizedHook`), `message_thread_id` is correctly passed through all paths. The streaming think-block + final answer scenario lacked focused regression coverage, which this test adds to prove the thread context is preserved.

## Summary

- Issue #89352 reports that Telegram forum topic `message_thread_id` can be dropped when a reply involves streamed reasoning followed by a final answer
- Codebase investigation found all current delivery paths already preserve `message_thread_id` correctly; the gap was in test coverage for the combined reasoning-stream + final-delivery scenario in forum topics
- Adds a focused regression test covering forum thread context (`threadSpec: { id: 88, scope: "forum" }`) with both `onReasoningStream` and `deliver({ kind: "final" })`
- Asserts `message_thread_id` propagation at the `createTelegramDraftStream` boundary for both answer and reasoning lanes, not only at the prompt-context record
- Fixes #89352

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` — 130 passed (1 new, 0 failed)
- `pnpm check:test-types` — passed
- Autoreview: clean, no actionable findings
- CI: all green

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram forum topic `message_thread_id` being dropped when bot reply involves streamed reasoning (`<think>...</think>`) followed by final answer delivery.

Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22, branch `fix/issue-89352-telegram-forum-topic-streaming` at commit `b0665e74fa`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "preserves forum topic"
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "preserves forum topic"
> ...
 ✓ extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts (1 test) 1 passed

$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
> ...
 ✓ extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts (130 tests) 130 passed

  Tests  130 passed (130)

$ pnpm check:test-types
> ...
✔ tsgo test:types passed
```

Observed result after fix: The new test `preserves forum topic message_thread_id across streamed reasoning and final answer` passes. Both draft lanes (answer and reasoning) are verified to receive the correct `thread: { id: 88, scope: "forum" }` at the `createTelegramDraftStream` boundary, preventing regression where `message_thread_id` could be lost between the prompt-context record and the actual send path.

What was not tested: No live Telegram E2E test was performed (requires real Telegram bot token and forum group); the regression test covers the dispatch layer behavior with mocked Telegram context. The assertion was strengthened to the draft-stream boundary per ClawSweeper review finding.
target.author: xialonglee
